authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare. Its claims processing manual is the official public source for claim submission, processing, payment, remittance, correction, and reopening workflows that constrain RCM queue sequencing and root-cause claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Revenue cycle intake, claim-state assessment, denial prioritization, and correction boundary handling
used_for: refusal when claim action lacks authoritative support

authoritative_sources.title: Prior Authorization and Pre-Claim Review Initiatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization and pre-claim review guidance is official payer operating guidance for services where authorization or documentation status gates claim submission and can shift RCM work to access or utilization owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, referral, pre-claim, and documentation prerequisite handling
used_for: routing to access, authorization, referral, or utilization owners

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Payment and Remittance Advice and Electronic Funds Transfer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administrative simplification guidance is authoritative for ERA, EFT, CARC, RARC, and remittance concepts that RCM leaders use when prioritizing denial, cash-posting, and reconciliation queues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Remittance, denial, cash-posting, AR, and reconciliation constraint assessment

authoritative_sources.title: First Level of Appeal: Redetermination by a Medicare Contractor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS redetermination guidance is official payer workflow authority for when denial work requires a formal appeal, MAC ownership, written request evidence, and timeframe controls rather than routine queue prioritization.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Denial escalation, appeal-window triage, and formal appeal boundary handling
used_for: appeal timing and documentation dependency awareness

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative workflow rules for eligibility, claim status, payment, and remittance transactions and normalize payer-provider evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, claim-status, payment, and remittance transaction workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Standardizing denial metrics for the revenue cycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFMA is a recognized healthcare finance professional association; this public guidance defines denial metric standardization, MAP KPI references, denial categories, and measurement principles used for RCM prioritization and root-cause work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Denial trend analysis, root-cause split, KPI selection, and priority calibration
used_for: front-end versus back-end denial category routing
revenue cycle KPI and benchmarking terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is authoritative for HIPAA Privacy Rule guidance. This source constrains RCM evidence handling by defining payment and healthcare operations uses and their privacy limits.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, payment or operations purpose check, and privacy escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI access, use, disclosure, and request scope in RCM prioritization and escalation outputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Minimum-necessary evidence packaging and privacy-sensitive refusal
used_for: PHI minimization in queue and escalation packets

authoritative_sources.title: HEAT Provider Compliance Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG provider compliance training is public federal guidance for healthcare fraud and abuse awareness, compliance program basics, documentation importance, and escalation when compliance issues arise in provider revenue workflows.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Tenant priority rules, capacity envelopes, payer, plan, facility, service setting, eligibility, referral, authorization or pre-claim status, documentation state, claim state, denial category, remittance state, timely-filing or appeal window, payer hierarchy, approval thresholds, and PHI handling constraints gate RCM prioritization and allocation recommendations.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The truth set comes from tenant source-of-truth mappings across EHR, registration, scheduling, practice management, eligibility, referral, authorization, coding, charge capture, billing, clearinghouse, payer portal, claim-status, remittance, denial-management, AR, collections, cash-posting, document, approval, audit, and RCM command-center dashboard systems.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at administrative evidence sufficiency, queue prioritization, constraint identification, root-cause split, and escalation packaging. Licensed clinical and coding owners decide diagnoses, treatment, medical necessity, level of care, code selection, modifiers, and clinical urgency. Privacy or compliance owners decide non-routine disclosure and privacy disputes. Payer-policy, authorization, appeal, legal, finance, accounting, staffing, and executive owners make final interpretations and approvals.

workflow_stages.name: Demand and work intake
workflow_stages.description: Normalize the requested RCM planning question, identify affected work queues, patient/member or account context, payer and plan, service setting, tenant priority rule, approval owner, and source systems before prioritization begins.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, coding-support, claim, remittance, denial, AR, cash-posting, collections, privacy, and approval prerequisites that can change queue order or owner routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess work-queue volume, aging, denial trend, cash impact, deadlines, productivity, staffing capacity, owner availability, automation limits, approval gates, and patient/member risk signals.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Root-cause split and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Classify constraints as access, eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, coding, billing, payer-processing, denial, remittance, AR, collections, cash-posting, privacy, compliance, finance, or approval driven without taking adjacent authority.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and queue sequence recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Recommend queue ordering and corrective-work sequence using approved rules, evidence, capacity, deadlines, expected cash impact, denial leakage, and downstream effects while preserving approval boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Refresh the recommendation when queue, payer, capacity, source, or policy facts change and escalate when clinical, payer, privacy, compliance, finance, legal, staffing, write-off, refund, or final approval authority is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: access, registration, scheduling, eligibility, referral, and authorization work-queue reports
coding, documentation, charge capture, and billing edit reports
denial inventory, CARC and RARC, payer correspondence, appeal-window, and root-cause summaries
AR aging, collections, patient-balance, cash-posting, 835 ERA, EFT, EOB, and remittance reports
payer mix, service-line, facility, productivity, capacity, leakage, and expected cash-impact summaries
tenant priority matrix, source-of-truth hierarchy, approval matrix, work-queue owner map, and privacy channel rules
RCM prioritization briefs, constraint logs, allocation recommendations, and cross-lane escalation notes
systems_of_record: registration, scheduling, patient-access, eligibility, referral, and authorization systems
coding, charge capture, claim scrubber, billing, and clearinghouse systems
payer portals, claim-status transactions, and payer correspondence repositories
AR follow-up, collections, cash-posting, remittance, and patient-ledger systems
document repositories, policy repositories, approval systems, and audit logs

allowed: Recommend queue ordering based on approved tenant rules, aging, denial trends, cash impact, deadlines, upstream blockers, owner capacity, and documented risk signals.
Determine likely root-cause lane for a variance using retrieved source records and source-of-truth hierarchy.
Produce constraint logs, cross-lane escalation notes, and refresh recommendations.
Identify which prerequisite must move first before downstream billing, denial, AR, posting, or collections work can proceed.
escalate: Escalate staffing, overtime, budget, write-off, refund, waiver, collections, policy exception, appeal, or operational change approvals.
Escalate code selection, modifier decisions, documentation changes, diagnosis support, medical necessity, level of care, clinical urgency, or patient-safety judgment.
Escalate payer-policy interpretation, authorization approval, appeal certification, privacy approval, compliance signoff, accounting treatment, tax, legal, or executive decisions.
Escalate when tenant facts, source-of-truth hierarchy, payer rules, public sources, or system records materially conflict.
refuse: Refuse to present recommendations as final approval.
Refuse to infer eligibility, referral, authorization, coverage, documentation adequacy, payer hierarchy, medical necessity, coding, or appeal position from incomplete records.
Refuse uncontrolled PHI disclosure or full-record export outside approved minimum-necessary channels.
Refuse unsupported changes to claim, ledger, collections, write-off, refund, policy, staffing, budget, or clinical disposition.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Front-end denial growth misrouted as billing execution
domain_failure_modes.impact: Revenue leakage, repeated denials, timely-filing exposure, and failure to remove upstream eligibility, referral, or authorization blockers.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI overexposure in queue or escalation artifacts
domain_failure_modes.impact: Privacy violation, incomplete audit trail, and unnecessary disclosure of patient or member data.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Planning recommendation treated as final approval
domain_failure_modes.impact: Unauthorized staffing, budget, write-off, refund, collections, policy, or operational changes.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Clinical, coding, or payer-policy interpretation implied by administrative planning
domain_failure_modes.impact: Patient or member harm, claim denial, compliance exposure, and inappropriate substitution for licensed or payer-authorized judgment.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Conflicting systems or policies synthesized without authority
domain_failure_modes.impact: Incorrect queue order, unsupported appeal or correction path, compliance exposure, and disputed owner routing.
Generic or unsafe RCM behavior that ignores healthcare evidence, PHI, payer, clinical, and approval boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Denial growth is being caused by a front-end issue rather than billing execution
Two revenue lanes disagree about ownership of a variance
A high-impact revenue fix would require unsupported policy or staffing approval
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific payer contract, state Medicaid, MAC, and commercial payer requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Public sources establish the baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant payer, plan, jurisdiction, and source-of-truth context before final queue recommendations that depend on these rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact tenant capacity, productivity, and approval thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires tenant capacity and approval facts before deterministic allocation, and scenarios require escalation when those facts are absent.

source_classes_covered: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
market_ready_research_conclusion.conclusion: Research is sufficient for a validated specification-based healthcare RCM prioritization package when tenant-specific facts and payer rules are retrieved at runtime.
